description_long: This object is a cylindrical stone block with a rough, weathered surface, displaying a mix of gray, white, and dark mottled patches. The top face is flat and slightly uneven, with visible surface abrasions and a small rectangular label or sticker affixed to it. The sides of the block show irregular discoloration, likely from exposure to the elements, and there are some visible markings, including numbers and symbols, possibly for identification or cataloging. One side features a distinct patch of lighter stone, contrasting with the darker, more stained areas. The block appears solid and heavy, with no visible openings or recesses. Its utilitarian appearance suggests it is used as a barrier or obstacle in urban or construction environments.
description: A cylindrical, weathered stone block with mottled gray and white coloration, surface markings, and a flat top. It appears solid, heavy, and utilitarian, likely used as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: The front shows a mottled gray and white cylindrical surface with a flat, slightly rough top and a visible label.
description_view_1: This side is darker overall, with less visible contrast and the label on the top just visible at the edge.
description_view_2: A lighter patch and a yellowish marking with numbers are visible, along with more pronounced surface texture.
description_view_3: This view highlights handwritten black markings and a lighter, more uniform stone surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or roadside as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: The surface shows significant discoloration and mottling, likely from prolonged outdoor exposure, with some areas appearing more worn or stained than others.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: There are visible handwritten numbers and symbols, as well as a small rectangular label affixed to the top.
